task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Support Triage (AI-assisted)** (Level 9)

Build a support ticket triage system. Tickets have subject, body, from_email, category, priority, status. Use a Claude agent named 'Triage' that receives a ticket and returns a JSON object with {category, priority}. When a new ticket is POSTed, call the Triage agent to classify it before saving. Need: POST /api/tickets (auto-classifies), GET /api/tickets (list), GET /api/tickets/open.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for web
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend
database is local memory

create a Tickets table:
  subject, required
  body
  from_email
  category, default 'General'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  status, default 'open'

owner is 'admin@example.com'
allow signup and login

agent 'Triage' receives ticket:
  response = ask claude 'Analyze this support ticket and classify it. Return JSON with category (e.g. Bug, Feature Request, Account, Billing, General) and priority (e.g. Low, Normal, High, Critical). Be concise.' with ticket returning JSON text:
    category
    priority
  send back response

when user calls POST /api/tickets sending ticket_data:
  requires login
  validate ticket_data:
    subject is text, required
    from_email is text
  classified = call 'Triage' with ticket_data
  set ticket_data's category to classified's category
  set ticket_data's priority to classified's priority
  saved = save ticket_data to Tickets
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/tickets:
  tickets = get all Tickets
  send back tickets

when user calls GET /api/tickets/open:
  open_tickets = get all Tickets where status is 'open'
  send back open_tickets

page 'Inbox' at '/':
  requires login
  section 'Submit Ticket':
    subject = '' as a text input
    body = '' as a text area
    from_email = '' as a text input
    button 'Submit Ticket':
      send {subject: subject, body: body, from_email: from_email} as a new Ticket to '/api/tickets'
      on page load get tickets from '/api/tickets/open'
  on page load get tickets from '/api/tickets/open'
  section 'Open Support Tickets':
    display tickets as table
